label: Domain Modeling
summary: Types and domain objects that hide business intent or invalid states.

rules.label: Rust Debug Secret
rules.description: Debug is derived on a type that carries secret-like fields.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Debug Secret matters because the pattern here, Debug is derived on a type that carries secret-like fields, hides a sensitive edge where convenience shortcuts can become security or data-handling incidents.
rules.fix: For Rust Debug Secret in Rust, pull the sensitive value from env, config, or a secret manager instead of embedding it directly in source.

rules.label: Rust Domain Default Produces Invalid
rules.description: Default is derived or implemented on a type that likely cannot have a safe default state.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Domain Default Produces Invalid matters because the pattern here, Default is derived or implemented on a type that likely cannot have a safe default state, is worth review because rust gives you strong tools to encode intent, and weak modeling throws that advantage away.
rules.fix: For Rust Domain Default Produces Invalid in Rust, model the real business state directly in the types, and keep impossible or unsupported states out of normal flows.

rules.label: Rust Domain Float for Money
rules.description: Floating-point storage is used for money-like values.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Domain Float for Money matters because the pattern here, Floating-point storage is used for money-like values, is worth review because rust gives you strong tools to encode intent, and weak modeling throws that advantage away.
rules.fix: For Rust Domain Float for Money in Rust, model the real business state directly in the types, and keep impossible or unsupported states out of normal flows.

rules.label: Rust Domain Impossible Combination
rules.description: A boolean toggle is mixed with optional credentials, creating invalid-state combinations.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Domain Impossible Combination matters because the pattern here, A boolean toggle is mixed with optional credentials, creating invalid-state combinations, is worth review because rust gives you strong tools to encode intent, and weak modeling throws that advantage away.
rules.fix: For Rust Domain Impossible Combination in Rust, model the real business state directly in the types, and keep impossible or unsupported states out of normal flows.

rules.label: Rust Domain Optional Secret Default
rules.description: A defaultable type includes optional secret-like fields, which can hide invalid configuration.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Domain Optional Secret Default matters because the pattern here, A defaultable type includes optional secret-like fields, which can hide invalid configuration, hides a sensitive edge where convenience shortcuts can become security or data-handling incidents.
rules.fix: For Rust Domain Optional Secret Default in Rust, pull the sensitive value from env, config, or a secret manager instead of embedding it directly in source.

rules.label: Rust Domain Raw Primitive
rules.description: Business-facing data is stored as a raw primitive instead of a stronger domain type.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Domain Raw Primitive matters because the pattern here, Business-facing data is stored as a raw primitive instead of a stronger domain type, shows that the code is bypassing a clearer or safer default that readers usually expect to see.
rules.fix: For Rust Domain Raw Primitive in Rust, prefer a stronger domain type when the semantics match, and keep the expected or cheaper API choice as the default.

rules.label: Rust Serde Sensitive Deserialize
rules.description: Deserialize is derived for sensitive fields without obvious validation.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Serde Sensitive Deserialize matters because the pattern here, Deserialize is derived for sensitive fields without obvious validation, removes a guardrail or signal that reviewers expect before trusting the surrounding code.
rules.fix: For Rust Serde Sensitive Deserialize in Rust, add obvious validation or move the code to a path where that safeguard is guaranteed before the risky work happens.

rules.label: Rust Serde Sensitive Serialize
rules.description: Serialize is derived for secret-like fields that may need redaction or exclusion.
rules.explanation: In Rust, Rust Serde Sensitive Serialize matters because the pattern here, Serialize is derived for secret-like fields that may need redaction or exclusion, hides a sensitive edge where convenience shortcuts can become security or data-handling incidents.
rules.fix: For Rust Serde Sensitive Serialize in Rust, pull the sensitive value from env, config, or a secret manager instead of embedding it directly in source.
